title: Warhammer Quest - Let’s Play - Part 5
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kjl_jo_1HTg

Daniel (@dungeondive) continues the second dungeon delve as Harlan and Saul explore branching corridors in search of the fire chasm. The episode starts with a forgotten treasure draw—a Lifebringer spell scroll that sparks a rules debate about which warriors can use wizard-adjacent items. Daniel rules the witch hunter qualifies based on a careful reading of the treasure restrictions.

The dungeon exploration reveals dead ends and corridors with nothing in them, which Daniel defends as authentic simulation—not every passage holds danger, and that quiet tension is part of the game's appeal. Then the power phase delivers back-to-back ones: first goblin spearmen ambush the heroes on the stairs, and Harlan's bloodlust causes him to accidentally slash Saul for one wound. The barbarian redeems himself with a spectacular multi-deathblow chain. When the dust settles, the warriors reach the Flames of Kozma corridor—a rare healing tile that restores both heroes to full health. And then fate delivers the cruelest joke: the final unexplored path leads back to the bottomless pit. Saul stares across in disbelief. The objective room is on the other side.

----

Have you ever had a dungeon crawl where the map itself became your worst enemy—dead ends, backtracking, and the objective always just out of reach?
